explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode) and the inspiratory phase is active (inspiratoryPhase), then the inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp) must be equal to the target inspiratory pressure for the PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV). This captures the requirement that in PCV mode, the target inspiratory pressure is controlled by P_inspPCV, and by ensuring this condition holds continuously throughout the inspiratory phase, it ensures that the pressure is "kept constant" at that target level.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (PCVMode) and the inspiratory phase is active (inspiratoryPhase), then the inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp) must be equal to the target inspiratory pressure for PCV mode (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV). This accurately captures 'the target inspiratory pressure level shall be controlled by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV )' by ensuring P_insp is set to P_inspPCV when these conditions are met. The phrase 'and it is kept constant' is implicitly covered because the property demands that P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV holds for the entire duration that both PCVMode and inspiratoryPhase are true, meaning the pressure will not deviate from P_inspPCV during this time.

The natural language statement specifies two conditions for the PCV Mode: first, that the target inspiratory pressure level (P_insp) is controlled by the parameter P_inspPCV, and second, that this level is kept constant. The atomic proposition 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' directly captures the first part, indicating that the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure of the PCV mode. The phrase 'it is kept constant' implies that this condition must hold true continuously throughout the duration of the PCV mode. Therefore, the LTL formula 'G (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV)' expresses that 'globally' (G), whenever 'PCVMode' is active, it must always be the case that 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' is true. This ensures that the pressure is consistently set to and maintained at the P_inspPCV level during the PCV mode, thus capturing both aspects of being controlled by the parameter and being kept constant.

The property states that globally (G), whenever the system is in 'PCVMode' and the 'inspiratoryPhase' is active, the inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp) must be equal to its target inspiratory pressure for PCV mode (P_inspPCV). This precisely captures that the pressure is controlled by P_inspPCV. The 'kept constant' aspect is implicitly satisfied because the LTL formula requires the condition 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' to hold for every time step throughout the duration of the inspiratory phase while in PCV mode, thus ensuring the pressure remains at the specified constant level during this period.

This LTL property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'PCVMode' and the 'inspiratoryPhase' is active, it must always be true that the inspiratory valve pressure ('P_insp') is set to the target inspiratory pressure of the PCV mode ('P_inspPCV'). This effectively captures that the pressure is controlled by 'P_inspPCV' and is 'kept constant' at this value throughout the inspiratory phase while in PCV mode, as any deviation would violate the 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV' condition.

The LTL property "G (PCVMode & inspiratoryPhase -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV)" ensures that the inspiratory pressure level is controlled and kept constant as described. The "G" (Globally) operator indicates that this property must hold at all times. The antecedent "PCVMode & inspiratoryPhase" specifies the precise context: when the system is operating in Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) Mode and the inspiratory phase is active. The consequent "P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV" directly translates the requirement that the target inspiratory pressure (P_insp) must be set to the parameter P_inspPCV. By demanding that "P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV" holds true at every moment the inspiratory phase is active within PCV mode, the formula implicitly guarantees that the pressure level is "kept constant" throughout this period, as any fluctuation would cause the condition to be momentarily false, thus violating the global requirement.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'PCV Mode', it must be the case that during the 'inspiratoryPhase', the 'inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp)' is always equal to the 'target inspiratory pressure of the PCV mode (P_inspPCV)'. This captures that the inspiratory pressure level is controlled by the parameter and is kept constant throughout the inspiratory phase when in PCV mode.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must hold when the system is in 'PCV Mode'. Specifically, it states that the 'target inspiratory pressure level shall be controlled by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV ) and it is kept constant'. This implies that throughout the duration of the inspiratory phase, when the system is operating in PCV mode, the inspiratory pressure should match the predefined 'P_inspPCV' value and remain stable. The LTL formula `G (PCVMode & inspiratoryPhase -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV)` captures this by stating that 'Globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode and the inspiratory phase is active, then the inspiratory valve pressure (P_insp) must be equal to the target inspiratory pressure of the PCV mode (P_inspPCV)'. The 'G' operator ensures that this condition holds continuously whenever the antecedent (`PCVMode & inspiratoryPhase`) is true, thereby capturing the 'kept constant' aspect during the relevant period.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must hold when the system is in 'PCV Mode'. It states that 'The target inspiratory pressure level shall be controlled by the Inspiratory Pressure parameter (P_inspPCV ) and it is kept constant'. This translates to a global (G) implication: whenever 'PCVMode' is active, the inspiratory pressure (P_insp) must be equal to the target inspiratory pressure for PCV mode (P_inspPCV). The 'kept constant' part is captured by the fact that the equality must hold at all times while in PCV mode.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must hold when the system is in 'PCV Mode'. It states that the inspiratory pressure level 'shall be controlled by' the `P_inspPCV` parameter and 'it is kept constant'. The atomic proposition `PCVMode` indicates when the system is in Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode. The atomic proposition `P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV` indicates that the inspiratory valve pressure is set to the target inspiratory pressure of the PCV mode. The LTL formula `G (PCVMode -> P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV)` correctly captures this requirement. It means 'Globally (at all times), if the system is in PCV Mode, then the inspiratory pressure must be equal to the P_inspPCV value'. This ensures that the pressure is both controlled by the specified parameter and 'kept constant' (i.e., maintained at that value) for the entire duration of the PCV mode. If the pressure were to deviate while in PCV mode, the condition `P_insp_equalTo_P_inspPCV` would become false, violating the implication and thus the overall property.
